user_query:
I want you to set up a small, local-only expense log for early March and produce a clean export and summaries. Treat this as a personal finance recordkeeping task with a lightweight local ledger, but keep all working data in a project-local directory so nothing touches a home directory.

Input data (add ONLY the non-reimbursable items to the ledger):
- 2026-03-01 | Safeway | Groceries | -54.32 | Reimbursable: No
- 2026-03-02 | Uber | Airport ride | -37.80 | Reimbursable: No
- 2026-03-02 | Starbucks | Team coffee | -12.45 | Reimbursable: Yes
- 2026-03-03 | PG&E | Electric bill | -92.10 | Reimbursable: No
- 2026-03-04 | Netflix | Subscription | -15.49 | Reimbursable: No
- 2026-03-05 | Lyft | Client visit | -18.25 | Reimbursable: Yes
- 2026-03-06 | Whole Foods | Groceries | -76.88 | Reimbursable: No
- 2026-03-07 | Caltrain | Monthly pass | -87.00 | Reimbursable: No
- 2026-03-09 | Trader Joe's | Groceries | -34.16 | Reimbursable: No
- 2026-03-10 | Shell | Gasoline | -52.40 | Reimbursable: No
- 2026-03-11 | Blue Bottle | Coffee | -7.50 | Reimbursable: No

Category rules to apply to each non-reimbursable entry:
- Groceries: Safeway, Whole Foods, Trader Joe's
- Transport: Uber, Lyft, Caltrain, Shell
- Utilities: PG&E
- Entertainment: Netflix
- Dining: Starbucks, Blue Bottle
(If a merchant doesn’t match these, default to Misc — but all above should match.)

What to do:
1) For each non-reimbursable line (Reimbursable: No), add exactly one entry to a local ledger using a project-local data directory only (for example: .work/ledger). Do NOT write anything under a home directory. For the stored entry "value" text, use this exact format (including separators and brackets):
   "YYYY-MM-DD | Merchant | Amount | [Category]"
   Example: "2026-03-01 | Safeway | -54.32 | [Groceries]"
2) Export the ledger to CSV and ensure the exported file ends up at output/ledger-export.csv. The CSV must have the exact lowercase header: "timestamp,command,value" and then one row per added entry.
3) Create a normalized JSON of the posted (non-reimbursable) items at output/cleaned_expenses.json as an array of objects with fields: date (string), merchant (string), description (string), amount (number), category (string). Use the same 9 non-reimbursable items listed above with the mapped categories.
4) Create a category totals file at output/categories_summary.json with the following content, based on the 9 posted items:
   {
     "by_category": {
       "Groceries": -165.36,
       "Transport": -177.20,
       "Utilities": -92.10,
       "Entertainment": -15.49,
       "Dining": -7.50
     },
     "total": -457.65
   }
5) Write a short explanation at output/summary.md describing your approach (filtering out reimbursable entries, how categories were assigned, and how you ensured a local data directory). Include the exact lines:
   - "Excluded reimbursable entries: 2"
   - "Total posted: 9"

Important:
- Keep any runtime data for the ledger under a relative project-local directory only (e.g., .work/ledger). Do not use absolute paths or any home directory locations.
- Ensure the exported CSV header is exactly "timestamp,command,value" and that each value field follows the specified pipe-separated format with the category in square brackets.
